deep_dive.title: Multi-Agent Firewall Architecture for Privacy Protection of Sensitive Data in Interactions with Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have become essential productivity tools, their integration into workflows without adequate safeguards creates significant risks. This paper proposes an open-source, privacy-focused, user-facing firewall designed to secure both web-based and programmatic LLM interactions. The architecture combines a browser extension and a proxy for total traffic interception across both HTTP(S) and WebSocket communications. At its core, a flexible multi-agent pipeline delivers data leakage prevention through a hybrid approach combining deterministic detectors with LLM-driven semantic analysis, proprietary code leakage prevention, and extensible components designed for future security enhancements such as prompt injection evasion. The framework's layered architecture enables deployment across heterogeneous environments, allowing organizations to balance computational cost, detection depth and latency. Evaluation results demonstrate it achieves F1 scores of up to 94.93% on optimal configurations.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Privacy protection for sensitive data in LLM interactions via multi-agent firewall
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过多代理防火墙保护LLM交互中的敏感数据隐私
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过多代理防火墙保护LLM交互中的敏感数据隐私
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过多代理防火墙保护LLM交互中的敏感数据隐私
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an open-source multi-agent firewall architecture combining browser extension and proxy to prevent data leakage in LLM interactions.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出一种开源、注重隐私的多代理防火墙架构，用于保护用户与LLM交互时的敏感数据。架构结合浏览器扩展和代理，实现HTTP(S)和WebSocket全流量拦截。核心是多代理管道，融合确定性检测器和LLM驱动的语义分析，防止数据泄露。还包含专有代码泄露防护和可扩展的安全增强组件。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出一种开源、注重隐私的多代理防火墙架构，用于保护用户与LLM交互时的敏感数据。架构结合浏览器扩展和代理，实现HTTP(S)和WebSocket全流量拦截。核心是多代理管道，融合确定性检测器和LLM驱动的语义分析，防止数据泄露。还包含专有代码泄露防护和可扩展的安全增强组件。

deep_dive.title: When Agents Go Rogue: Activation-Based Detection of Malicious Behaviors in Multi-Agent Systems
deep_dive.abstract: While enabling effective collaboration on complex tasks, LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) face critical security challenges due to vulnerabilities at the agent and interaction levels. Most existing MAS security defenses are built upon two core assumptions: semantically-explicit malicious attacks and explicit graph-based modeling of the MAS topology and agent-level interactions. In practice, real-world attacks are becoming more semantically stealthy, while MAS execution is typically asynchronous without the temporal alignment assumed by graph-based propagation models. To address these limitations, we propose AcMAS, an activation-based framework for malicious-behavior detection in MAS. By analyzing internal reasoning states in the activation space of local agents, AcMAS detects even stealthy attacks in a synchronization-robust fashion, without relying on explicit interaction graphs. Moreover, our activation analysis provides critical signals to guide AcMAS in restoring the functionality of compromised agents, rather than the disruptive agent isolation commonly used by the state-of-the-art methods. Comprehensive evaluation demonstrates that AcMAS significantly outperforms graph-based baselines against stealthy attacks, by +0.22 F1 in synchronous settings (0.94 vs. 0.72) and by +0.55 F1 in asynchronous settings (0.93 vs. 0.38), with generalization across diverse open-source LLM backbones, attack intensity, and MAS scale.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Detection of malicious behaviors in multi-agent systems using activation-based analysis
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于激活状态的多代理系统恶意行为检测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于激活状态的多代理系统恶意行为检测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于激活状态的多代理系统恶意行为检测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes AcMAS, an activation-based framework for detecting stealthy malicious behaviors in LLM-based multi-agent systems without assuming explicit attacks or graph topology.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出AcMAS框架，基于激活状态检测LLM多代理系统中的恶意行为。现有防御假设攻击语义显式或建模拓扑图，但实际攻击更隐蔽且执行异步。AcMAS通过分析代理内部推理状态，不受这些假设限制，能有效检测语义隐晦的攻击。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出AcMAS框架，基于激活状态检测LLM多代理系统中的恶意行为。现有防御假设攻击语义显式或建模拓扑图，但实际攻击更隐蔽且执行异步。AcMAS通过分析代理内部推理状态，不受这些假设限制，能有效检测语义隐晦的攻击。

deep_dive.title: Beware of Agentic Botnets: Scalable Untargeted Promptware Attacks via Universal and Transferable Adversarial HalluSquatting
deep_dive.abstract: The growing adoption of agentic LLM applications has introduced a new threat previously named as promptware. While prior work has established that adversaries can exploit direct channels to LLM applications to apply promptware under weak threat models, many applications do not provide any direct channels that could be exploited for prompt injection beyond the Internet. This raises a question: can attackers exploit LLM applications at scale without any direct channels in practical threat models? In this work, we show that the inherent tendency of LLMs to hallucinate resource identifiers can be exploited to amplify untargeted promptware attacks that pull adversarial prompts at scale and could be exploited to establish a botnet. We introduce adversarial hallucination squatting, a technique in which attackers identify trending resources (e.g., popular repositories, popular skills, etc.), compute the LLM distribution of hallucinations on the trending resource names, and preemptively register them to host adversarial prompts. By leveraging the predictability and transferability of hallucinations across foundational LLMs and to application layers, adversaries can significantly amplify the reach of untargeted promptware under weak threat models and establish a botnet by exploiting LLM applications to install a bot on the device that pulled the compromised hallucinated resource from the Inter. We empirically demonstrate that hallucinated resource generation occurs at high rates, up to 85% in repository cloning scenarios and up to 100% in skill installation, and that these hallucinations transfer between foundational models and different prompts. We demonstrate the practicality of adversarial hallucination squatting against various production LLM applications with integrated terminals in their set of tools, achieving remote tool execution and remote code execution.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: promptware attacks on agentic LLM applications as AI security threat
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对代理型LLM应用的提示软件攻击是AI安全威胁
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对代理型LLM应用的提示软件攻击是AI安全威胁
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对代理型LLM应用的提示软件攻击是AI安全威胁
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Exploits LLM hallucination of resource identifiers to launch scalable untargeted promptware attacks on agentic bots.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文指出，代理型LLM应用面临一种新的提示软件威胁。作者发现，LLM产生错误资源标识符的固有倾向可被利用来放大非定向提示软件攻击。通过通用可迁移的对抗性幻觉劫持，攻击者可以大规模拉取对抗性提示，从而实现对代理型僵尸网络的利用。这项研究揭示了AI系统安全的一个新攻击面。

deep_dive.title: Token-Flow Firewall: Semantic Runtime Auditing for Persistent AI Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Persistent AI agents extend large language models (LLMs) beyond single-turn interaction into long-lived software systems. Unlike traditional chat assistants, unsafe content in these agents can propagate through persistent state, reusable skills, and tool-mediated interactions, creating a substantially larger semantic attack surface. We observe that most security-critical interactions in such agents are transmitted through natural-language token flows, including memory updates, tool arguments, retrieved files, and inter-component communications. This observation enables a new security formulation: unsafe behavior can be intercepted as risky semantic flows before reaching privileged runtime sinks. Based on this insight, we propose TokenWall, a runtime defense framework that acts as a semantic firewall over agent token flows. TokenWall performs boundary-aware semantic auditing over these flows, constructing structured source-sink audit records, applying lightweight local inspection before execution, and selectively escalating ambiguous high-risk cases to stronger arbitration modules. Unlike prior approaches that rely on sparse auditing or remote large-model oversight, TokenWall enables full-coverage pre-execution mediation while reducing remote arbitration and latency. Experiments on CIK-Bench show that TokenWall reduces attack success rate to 12.5% while maintaining a 97.4% benign executable pass rate without human confirmation. TokenWall further introduces only 0.69 seconds of additional latency on benign cases, demonstrating that semantic runtime containment can achieve a practical security-utility trade-off for persistent AI agents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: runtime security auditing for persistent AI agents against semantic threats
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 持久AI代理的运行时安全审计，应对语义威胁
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 持久AI代理的运行时安全审计，应对语义威胁
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 持久AI代理的运行时安全审计，应对语义威胁
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes TokenWall, a runtime defense that intercepts risky semantic flows in persistent AI agents using natural-language token flows.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 持久AI代理将LLM扩展为长期运行的软件系统，不安全内容可通过持久状态、可复用技能和工具中介交互传播，形成更大的语义攻击面。论文观察到大多数安全关键交互通过自然语言令牌流传输，包括内存更新、工具参数、检索文件等。基于此，提出TokenWall运行时防御，在危险语义流到达特权运行时汇点前进行拦截。该框架为AI代理提供了一种新的语义运行时审计机制。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 持久AI代理将LLM扩展为长期运行的软件系统，不安全内容可通过持久状态、可复用技能和工具中介交互传播，形成更大的语义攻击面。论文观察到大多数安全关键交互通过自然语言令牌流传输，包括内存更新、工具参数、检索文件等。基于此，提出TokenWall运行时防御，在危险语义流到达特权运行时汇点前进行拦截。该框架为AI代理提供了一种新的语义运行时审计机制。

deep_dive.title: Coded Task Offloading for Fluid Computing: A Privacy-Aware Approach under D2D Networks
deep_dive.abstract: Fluid Computing aims to support distributed applications execution across heterogeneous cloud, edge, and device resources, motivating task execution mechanisms that adapt to dynamic and privacy-sensitive environments under runtime conditions. In this context, current task offloading schemes rarely address privacy risks and information leakage under adversarial execution settings; furthermore, most coded computing proposals focus on straggler mitigation without considering system-level objectives such as energy awareness. This paper proposes a coded task offloading scheme for D2D networks under stochastic task arrivals and queue-based dynamics. The proposal combines task offloading techniques with linear secret sharing schemes, where tasks are encoded into redundant shares to support threshold-based recovery, straggler mitigation, and privacy preservation while enhancing system performance. Then, we formulate a privacy-aware offloading problem that jointly optimizes delay and energy while penalizing the theoretical privacy leakage of coded tasks under noisy leakage observations. The problem is solved using a branch-and-bound solver alongside a lightweight heuristic scheduler, both of which are evaluated through a discrete-event simulator. Results show that coded offloading improves the delay--energy trade-off with respect to classical full and parallel offloading schemes, while the heuristic achieves near-optimal performance, outperforming baseline and state-of-the-art solvers. The results also show how privacy leakage penalties reshape offloading decisions, exposing an inherent delay--energy--privacy trade-off.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Coded task offloading for distributed cloud-edge-device computing with privacy
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向分布式云边端计算的编码任务卸载与隐私保护
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向分布式云边端计算的编码任务卸载与隐私保护
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向分布式云边端计算的编码任务卸载与隐私保护
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Privacy-aware coded task offloading scheme for fluid computing using linear secret sharing, balancing straggler mitigation and energy efficiency under D2D networks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 流动计算支持云、边缘和设备异构资源上的分布式应用执行，但现有任务卸载方案忽视隐私风险且缺乏能量感知。本文提出基于线性秘密共享的编码任务卸载方案，结合随机任务到达和队列动态，在D2D网络下同时处理隐私保护、延迟容忍和能耗优化，显著提升了系统安全性和能效。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 流动计算支持云、边缘和设备异构资源上的分布式应用执行，但现有任务卸载方案忽视隐私风险且缺乏能量感知。本文提出基于线性秘密共享的编码任务卸载方案，结合随机任务到达和队列动态，在D2D网络下同时处理隐私保护、延迟容忍和能耗优化，显著提升了系统安全性和能效。

quick_skim.title: Builder, Defender, Breaker: The Case Against Removing the Human from the AI-Driven Security Lifecycle
quick_skim.abstract: Artificial intelligence has spread across the whole of the security lifecycle. The same family of models now writes application code, hardens it, and probes it for weaknesses, so that a single generative substrate increasingly performs all three roles at once. Enthusiasm for this convergence tends to treat full autonomy as the natural end point of partial assistance. This article argues that it is not. When the system that builds an artifact is drawn from the same distribution as the systems that defend and test it, the three roles inherit a common set of blind spots, and the independence that makes verification meaningful is quietly lost. Removing the human does more than raise the automation level: it collapses the external oracle against which machine output is judged, outruns the point at which a person could intervene, hands adversaries a predictable and poisonable target, and dissolves the locus of accountability when something fails. Drawing on evidence from autonomous code generation, adversarial machine learning, software fault tolerance, and the first all-machine hacking tournaments, we argue that the human belongs in the loop not as a temporary scaffold but as a permanent structural requirement, and set out what a defensible division of labour between people and machines should preserve.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: discusses AI security lifecycle and the risk of full autonomy, arguing for human involvement
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 讨论人工智能安全生命周期和完全自动化的风险，主张人类参与
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 讨论人工智能安全生命周期和完全自动化的风险，主张人类参与
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 讨论人工智能安全生命周期和完全自动化的风险，主张人类参与
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Argues that removing humans from the AI-driven security lifecycle collapses external verification and introduces shared blind spots, advocating for human oversight.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文反对在人工智能驱动的安全生命周期中完全去除人类。当同一个模型家族同时承担构建、防御和测试角色时，它们共享盲点，导致验证的独立性丧失。作者认为，去除人类不仅提高了自动化水平，还破坏了安全验证的意义。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文反对在人工智能驱动的安全生命周期中完全去除人类。当同一个模型家族同时承担构建、防御和测试角色时，它们共享盲点，导致验证的独立性丧失。作者认为，去除人类不仅提高了自动化水平，还破坏了安全验证的意义。

quick_skim.title: AGL-1: The Enterprise AI Governance Layer as a Control Plane for Trusted Enterprise Intelligence
quick_skim.abstract: Enterprise artificial intelligence is moving from isolated experimentation toward operational dependency across copilots, retrieval-augmented generation systems, autonomous agents, and AI-enabled business workflows. As this transition accelerates, the primary enterprise challenge is no longer only model access or inference scale. It is governed intelligence operations: the ability to enforce authorization, preserve contextual lineage, control persistent memory, detect stale or conflicting knowledge, constrain agentic execution, and produce audit-ready evidence across distributed AI estates.   This paper introduces AGL-1, the Enterprise AI Governance Layer, as a vendor-neutral reference model for the control plane that should operate across foundation models, retrieval systems, orchestration frameworks, enterprise memory, policy engines, observability systems, tools, APIs, and business applications.   Building on governed knowledge-system principles introduced in GKS-5, AGL-1 generalizes the governance problem from retrieval-specific controls to full AI execution-path governance. It identifies recurring failure modes such as unauthorized retrieval, stale grounding, unmanaged memory, weak provenance, policy drift, fragmented observability, and uncontrolled autonomous execution. It then defines seven governance domains: identity-aware retrieval, policy enforcement, provenance management, memory governance, knowledge integrity monitoring, agentic execution control, and trust observability.   The central claim is that durable enterprise value from AI will increasingly depend on the ability to govern intelligence at scale. In complex enterprises, trust is not a property of the model alone. It is a property of the system around the model: identity, knowledge, policy, memory, tools, human oversight, and evidence working together as a managed control plane.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Enterprise AI governance layer - cloud security solutions
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 企业AI治理-云安全解决方案
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 企业AI治理-云安全解决方案
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 企业AI治理-云安全解决方案
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces AGL-1, a vendor-neutral reference model for enterprise AI governance, enforcing authorization, lineage, memory control, and audit across distributed AI systems.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 企业AI从实验转向运营依赖，面临授权、上下文血缘、持久内存、知识冲突检测、智能体行为约束和审计等治理挑战。本文提出AGL-1企业AI治理层，作为供应商中立的参考模型，为分布式AI资产提供控制平面，实现可信的企业智能运维。

quick_skim.title: Algorithmically Presented Numbers and Canonical Representations in Cryptographic Protocols
quick_skim.abstract: This paper develops a representation-theoretic perspective on cryptographic protocols. The focus is not solely on the computability of the abstract value as an extensional property, but on the algorithmic structure of its presentation in a representation system: for operational use in protocols, algorithmic accessibility of the value does not suffice; its fixed presentation is also decisive. We distinguish three representation-theoretic notions -- algorithmically approximable (A_app, the computable real numbers), finitely exactly describable in a system (A_fin(S)), and canonical normalizability of a system -- and show that there is no computable extensional canonicalizer that uniformly transforms arbitrary approximation programs of computable real numbers into unique finite value encodings. As the operational rational core presentation we use the rational system with its canonical encoding specification Sigma_Q (fixed rules for valid fraction descriptions, canonical codes, and normalization); the associated value set is A_ex = Q. The notion of a canonically serializable object class transfers this core idea to practical protocol objects (files as byte sequences, hash values, transaction IDs, and normatively serialized payloads). We illustrate the consequences for interoperability, well-definedness, and verification with fully worked toy examples from symmetric and asymmetric encryption and hashing, and with a real-world example, the snaproot hash-anchoring protocol for blockchain-based file integrity verification. The paper thereby shows that the mathematical determinacy of a value and its operational uniqueness as a protocol object are two different requirements. Once a normative representation specification has been fixed, byte-level correctness and well-definedness arguments can be carried out without further implementation-dependent serialization or rounding decisions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Representation-theoretic perspective on cryptographic protocols for key recovery and authentication
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 密码协议中表示论视角，涉及密钥恢复和身份认证
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 密码协议中表示论视角，涉及密钥恢复和身份认证
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 密码协议中表示论视角，涉及密钥恢复和身份认证
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Develops a representation-theoretic view of cryptographic protocols, distinguishing algorithmically approximable, finitely describable, and canonical normalizable numbers.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文从表示论角度分析密码协议，关注数值的算法结构而非可计算性。区分了算法可逼近数、系统内有限精确可描述数和系统可规范归一化三种概念，并证明不存在可计算的扩展规范器能统一转换任意逼近程序。该工作对密钥恢复和用户认证中数值表示的精确性有理论意义。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文从表示论角度分析密码协议，关注数值的算法结构而非可计算性。区分了算法可逼近数、系统内有限精确可描述数和系统可规范归一化三种概念，并证明不存在可计算的扩展规范器能统一转换任意逼近程序。该工作对密钥恢复和用户认证中数值表示的精确性有理论意义。

quick_skim.title: Measuring Healthcare Data Leaks and Security Flaws at Internet Scale
quick_skim.abstract: Systems that process medical data should be meticulously secured. Yet, network services in healthcare environments often fail to implement basic security measures. For example, previous studies showed that network segmentation flaws led to DICOM systems leaking millions of patient records.   In addition to DICOM, healthcare facilities rely heavily on the HL7 and FHIR protocols to transmit data. For nine months, we operated a low-interaction honeypot for medical protocols. We found it was regularly scanned for DICOM but never for HL7 or FHIR, indicating that despite their widespread use and importance for patient data security, the security of these services remains underexplored.   In this paper, we present the first large-scale study on HL7 and FHIR services and expand previous work on DICOM. Our large-scale Internet scans, covering the three major healthcare protocols across IPv4 and IPv6 address spaces, identify healthcare systems and uncover data leaks due to authentication flaws. Additionally, we scanned for deficiencies in TLS configurations of these services and known insecure healthcare software.   In total, we found \TotalEndpointsAuthFlaws ~healthcare services with authentication flaws. \NonTLSPercentage{}\% of all exposed systems do not support transport encryption, and \AllServerCVE{} systems have known software vulnerabilities, including those with potential for system takeover and CVSS scores up to 9.8. Overall, our study reveals an alarming state of cybersecurity in healthcare deployments, for which we discuss potential reasons and countermeasures. Finally, we report on the coordinated disclosure campaign we initiated to improve the security of patient data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Internet-scale measurement of healthcare data leaks and security flaws
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 互联网规模的医疗数据泄露和安全缺陷测量
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 互联网规模的医疗数据泄露和安全缺陷测量
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 互联网规模的医疗数据泄露和安全缺陷测量
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: First large-scale study of HL7 and FHIR protocol security in healthcare, revealing underexplored vulnerabilities and data leaks risks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文首次对医疗领域HL7和FHIR协议进行大规模安全研究。通过运行九个月的低交互蜜罐，发现DICOM被频繁扫描但HL7/FHIR无人问津，表明这些广泛使用的协议安全严重不足。研究揭示了医疗数据泄露的规模和安全缺陷的普遍性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文首次对医疗领域HL7和FHIR协议进行大规模安全研究。通过运行九个月的低交互蜜罐，发现DICOM被频繁扫描但HL7/FHIR无人问津，表明这些广泛使用的协议安全严重不足。研究揭示了医疗数据泄露的规模和安全缺陷的普遍性。

quick_skim.title: Trustworthy Machine Learning through the Lens of Combinatorial Optimization: Survey and Research Perspectives
quick_skim.abstract: Modern machine learning (ML) increasingly relies on complex models whose behavior is difficult to characterize beyond empirical performance metrics. Across a wide range of tasks, including prediction, generation, and decision-making, models with similar empirical performance can exhibit markedly different properties in terms of their transparency, interpretability, robustness, fairness, privacy, and certifiability. This survey highlights how optimization- and certification-oriented reasoning can provide a useful framework for reasoning about such differences, supporting tasks ranging from model training and selection to auditing and certification. We review and synthesize recent advances at the intersection of combinatorial optimization (CO) and trustworthy ML, covering both training and post-training tasks, including interpretable model learning, explanation generation, robustness analysis, fairness auditing, model compression, and privacy attacks and protections. Across these domains, CO formulations offer additional capabilities over purely heuristic approaches, e.g., gradient-based ones, notably global guarantees, formal certificates, and explicit treatment of trade-offs. While scalability remains an important challenge, continued progress in solvers and hybrid algorithms suggests a growing role for CO in the design and deployment of trustworthy ML systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: survey on trustworthy ML covering robustness, privacy, fairness
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 综述可信机器学习，涵盖鲁棒性、隐私、公平性等AI安全主题
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 综述可信机器学习，涵盖鲁棒性、隐私、公平性等AI安全主题
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 综述可信机器学习，涵盖鲁棒性、隐私、公平性等AI安全主题
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This survey reviews how combinatorial optimization aids trustworthy ML, addressing transparency, robustness, privacy, and certifiability.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文综述了组合优化在可信机器学习中的应用，从透明度、鲁棒性、隐私、可证明性等角度分析了优化推理如何支持模型训练、选择和认证，为AI安全研究提供系统框架。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文综述了组合优化在可信机器学习中的应用，从透明度、鲁棒性、隐私、可证明性等角度分析了优化推理如何支持模型训练、选择和认证，为AI安全研究提供系统框架。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: artificial intelligence security threats and defenses

quick_skim.title: Privilege and confidentiality in generative AI workflows
quick_skim.abstract: Generative AI (GenAI) systems store and process client data in three distinct ways: in the model's parameters through training and memorisation, in the context window during a live session, and in knowledge databases for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). Each mode creates different and often counter-intuitive risks to confidentiality and legal professional privilege, and each calls for specific governance responses. Drawing on the first English and American decisions to address privilege and generative AI, UK and Munir v Secretary of State for the Home Department and United States v Heppner, on the orthodox privilege authorities against which those decisions must be read, and on recent computer science research, we explain the three modes of data storage and processing in terms accessible to practitioners and analyse the legal consequences of each. We then situate the analysis within the regulatory framework governing solicitors in England and Wales and within the ordinary principles of professional negligence, arguing that the standard of effective information governance (and with it the benchmark against which negligence and misconduct will be measured) is changing. Although we write primarily for SRA-regulated practitioners, our data-governance analysis is framed to extend to any jurisdiction in which the protection of privilege or professional secrecy depends on demonstrable confidentiality. The ultimate aim of this article is to help legal services professionals understand salient data leakage risks in GenAI systems and thereby facilitate a more responsible deployment of GenAI on client data and other sensitive material.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 生成式AI工作流中的保密性
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 生成式AI工作流中的保密性
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 生成式AI工作流中的保密性
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Analyzes confidentiality and legal privilege risks in three GenAI data storage modes, drawing on recent legal decisions to inform governance.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文分析了生成式AI系统中三种数据存储模式（模型参数、上下文窗口、知识数据库）带来的保密性和法律特免权风险。通过引用英美最新判例和计算机科学研究，提出了针对每种风险的治理建议。该工作凸显了AI安全中隐私与权限管理的关键挑战。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文分析了生成式AI系统中三种数据存储模式（模型参数、上下文窗口、知识数据库）带来的保密性和法律特免权风险。通过引用英美最新判例和计算机科学研究，提出了针对每种风险的治理建议。该工作凸显了AI安全中隐私与权限管理的关键挑战。

quick_skim.title: TRM-Raft: A Byzantine-Resistant Raft Consensus via Integrated Trust and Reputation Model
quick_skim.abstract: Internetware envisions autonomous software entities collaborating over the open Internet. Raft consensus is widely adopted for its simplicity and performance in distributed coordination, e.g., service registries and blockchains. However, Raft assumes crash faults only, making it vulnerable to Byzantine behaviors like election forgery and log tampering. Existing BFT protocols incur high overhead, while ad-hoc hardening lacks unified defense.   We propose \textbf{TRM-Raft}, a Byzantine-resistant enhancement that non-intrusively integrates a Blockchain-based Trust and Reputation Model (B-TRM) into the consensus core. It quantifies multi-dimensional node behaviors, applies adaptive penalties distinguishing accidental faults from malice, and embeds reputation into leader election and log replication. A reputation-aware election penalizes term/index forgery, excluding low-reputation nodes from leadership. A Schnorr-signature-based mechanism lets followers verify log integrity; tampering triggers reputation decay and leader replacement. Evaluated on Hyperledger Fabric in a realistic Internetware setting, TRM-Raft keeps malicious leader ratio below 5\% even with 40\% Byzantine nodes, with <10\% throughput loss and <5\% latency increase over vanilla Raft. TRM-Raft offers a lightweight, practical trustworthiness path for Internetware systems relying on Raft.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Byzantine-resistant Raft consensus for distributed systems
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 分布式系统中抵抗拜占庭错误的Raft共识
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 分布式系统中抵抗拜占庭错误的Raft共识
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 分布式系统中抵抗拜占庭错误的Raft共识
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Enhances Raft consensus with a blockchain-based trust and reputation model to resist Byzantine attacks in distributed coordination.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出TRM-Raft，通过集成基于区块链的信任与声誉模型，增强Raft共识的抗拜占庭能力。该方法非侵入式地量化节点行为，自适应区分偶然故障和恶意行为，并在宕机恢复后维持声誉记忆。实验表明TRM-Raft在保持Raft低开销的同时，有效防御选举伪造和日志篡改等攻击。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出TRM-Raft，通过集成基于区块链的信任与声誉模型，增强Raft共识的抗拜占庭能力。该方法非侵入式地量化节点行为，自适应区分偶然故障和恶意行为，并在宕机恢复后维持声誉记忆。实验表明TRM-Raft在保持Raft低开销的同时，有效防御选举伪造和日志篡改等攻击。

quick_skim.title: Agentic Data Environments
quick_skim.abstract: Autonomous agents promise substantial gains in speed, scale, and labor efficiency, but their failures can impose abrupt and often irreversible costs. The central challenge for agentic automation is therefore to increase the benefits of automation while bounding the consequences of failure.   While databases remain central to modern computing, agents operate over a broader data environment spanning files, APIs, applications, and system state. In this talk, I will outline early work on Agentic Data Environments -- the execution substrate in which agents operate -- that both amplify agent capabilities and enforce safety guarantees. This perspective reframes data systems from passive stores of state into active substrates for safe, reliable execution.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Safe execution and security guarantees for agentic automation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 代理自动化的安全执行与安全保证
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 代理自动化的安全执行与安全保证
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 代理自动化的安全执行与安全保证
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Agentic Data Environments as an execution substrate that amplifies agent capabilities while enforcing safety guarantees.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出代理数据环境（Agentic Data Environments）概念，将数据系统从被动存储转变为主动执行基板，在增强自主代理能力的同时强制安全保证。核心目标是平衡自动化的收益与失败后果。该框架涵盖文件、API、应用和系统状态，为安全可靠的代理执行提供基础设施。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出代理数据环境（Agentic Data Environments）概念，将数据系统从被动存储转变为主动执行基板，在增强自主代理能力的同时强制安全保证。核心目标是平衡自动化的收益与失败后果。该框架涵盖文件、API、应用和系统状态，为安全可靠的代理执行提供基础设施。

quick_skim.title: Modeling Failure Dynamics in Time-Constrained Authentication Systems: Evidence of a Success Cliff in USSD Workflows
quick_skim.abstract: Time-constrained interactive systems such as USSD (Unstructured Supplementary Service Data)-based financial services operate under strict session limits and sequential user interaction. While stronger authentication mechanisms improve security, they also increase interaction complexity and time burden, potentially reducing transaction completion. In this work, we model the failure dynamics of such systems and investigate how authentication complexity interacts with user response time and network round-trip time to influence session success rate. We propose and implement a simulation-based framework to investigate these failure dynamics and formally define a non-linear failure phenomenon, termed the \textit{Success Cliff}, where session success rates sharply decline beyond a critical complexity threshold. Through controlled experiments, we quantify the trade-off between security and usability and identify conditions under which secure authentication workflows become operationally unreliable.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: models failure dynamics in time-constrained authentication systems, relevant to identity authentication
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 模拟时间受限认证系统中的故障动态，与身份认证相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 模拟时间受限认证系统中的故障动态，与身份认证相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 模拟时间受限认证系统中的故障动态，与身份认证相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Models authentication failure dynamics in USSD systems, revealing a non-linear success cliff due to complexity and time constraints.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 时间受限的交互系统如USSD金融服务面临认证复杂度与成功率之间的权衡。本文建立了故障动力学模型，并定义了成功悬崖现象：随着认证复杂度增加，会话成功率呈现非线性下降。通过仿真框架验证了用户响应时间和网络延迟对失败的影响，为设计安全且可用的认证机制提供了洞见。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 时间受限的交互系统如USSD金融服务面临认证复杂度与成功率之间的权衡。本文建立了故障动力学模型，并定义了成功悬崖现象：随着认证复杂度增加，会话成功率呈现非线性下降。通过仿真框架验证了用户响应时间和网络延迟对失败的影响，为设计安全且可用的认证机制提供了洞见。
